highlights.text: I stated yesterday “My lean is we fill it out then we breakout.

Institutions leave their footprint on the chart when they long, because they do so the same way everytime: They wait for a deep elevator down sell, they wait for price to undercut a low, they then trap shorts, price recovers the low, and we squeeze the other way.
This is the Failed Breakdown, and my edge is simply following them.
In addition, the size of the squeeze is roughly proportionate to the elevator down sell that precedes it.
To get more upside though, institutions would need to recharge and they do so by trapping another batch of shorts via a Failed Breakdown.
I wrote on Tuesday at 4pm: “The Failed Breakdown of the 11:30AM 6818 low is also actionable.” We got exactly this Wednesday - ES went elevator down from 6865 resistance down to 6817.
ES went elevator down from 6877 down to 6836.
I gave this entry Wednesday at 4pm: “A possible long is if we hold 6837 and recover 6841.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into some of the aforementioned Failed Breakdowns we saw this week (these are key to learn from) and I’ll talk the actionable plan for Monday with entries.

post_title: SPX Racks Up Two Weeks With Only One Red Day. Will It Run Out of Steam Next Week? Dec 8 Plan
